intent: amos-protocol is public ALPHA, so an on-chain Anchor compile must not gate ordinary docs/relay/agent changes. This PR does three things so main becomes greenable for ordinary work and the plumbline pin can land: (1) On-chain CI is advisory, not gating. The Anchor Build job already has continue-on-error:true and the 'Anchor Build (on-chain programs)' check is deliberately NOT in .plumbline/policy.json ci_evidence_checks, so the Plumbline gate never requires it. I strengthened the ci.yml comment to state the alpha=advisory-on-chain posture explicitly and record how to flip it back to gating (add to ci_evidence_checks + drop continue-on-error) when the on-chain programs re-enter active change. Program IDs are frozen, so nothing of value is unguarded. (2) Greened the real 'Lint & Unit Tests' failure: three clippy useless_borrows_in_formatting errors in amos-agent/src/bedrock.rs (lines 124/134/145) that failed under -D warnings. Minimal fix — dropped the redundant & on Display args inside format!; no logic change (the AWS SigV4 canonical request/signature output is byte-identical). This is the same class as the 2.0 clippy baseline, not a refactor. (3) Pinned the Plumbline gate from amos-labs/plumbline@master to the immutable released tag @v0.3.0 in both the full-gate 'uses:' step and the no-key 'npx' fallback, closing the 'consumers float on master' finding; v0.3.0's fail-closed require_semantic_review default is safe here since this repo runs review under a configured ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

result_summary: Three files changed. amos-agent/src/bedrock.rs: removed redundant & on three format! Display args (clippy useless_borrows_in_formatting under -D warnings) — the real cause of the red 'Lint & Unit Tests', no behavior change to SigV4 signing. .github/workflows/ci.yml: strengthened the Anchor Build job comment to state on-chain CI is advisory (not gating) while the protocol is alpha — the job keeps continue-on-error:true and the check stays out of ci_evidence_checks, so it runs informationally but never blocks a merge; documented how to re-arm it. .github/workflows/plumbline.yml: pinned the gate from @master to immutable @v0.3.0 (full 'uses:' step + npx fallback). Verified locally: full-workspace clippy clean, fmt clean, both workflows parse. Ci-evidence for the greened Lint check is corroborated by this PR's own gate run. Touches .github/workflows/** and .plumbline/** (both protected) so self_modifying:true; the gate escalates to human REVIEW by design — expected, do not auto-merge.
